text: Regulatory and trade policy developments add another layer of uncertainty. President Trump is preparing industry-specific tariffs alongside country-by-country duties expected to take effect by August 1, 2025, which could impact Apple's global supply chain and product pricing. While G-20 finance ministers committed to international policy cooperation amid escalating trade tensions, the tariff environment remains a key risk factor. Analyst commentary indicates iPhone demand has been propped up by pull-ahead buying ahead of potential tariff implementation, though this may create demand headwinds in subsequent quarters.
